title: acaena sanguisorbae subsp. novae-zelandiae
intro: Acaena sanguisorbae subsp. novae-zelandiae, also known as New Zealand bugweed or red signal grass, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive red or yellow flowers and a sprawling habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Acaena sanguisorbae subsp. novae-zelandiae include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
